assertions.assertion: Response mentions updating @using IdentityServer4 directives in .cshtml files or _ViewImports.cshtml to @using Duende.IdentityServer
assertions.rationale: Explicitly mentions updating _ViewImports.cshtml from @using IdentityServer4 to @using Duende.IdentityServer.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions that Duende templates use Razor Pages instead of MVC controllers, or that the UI layout has changed significantly
assertions.rationale: Does not mention Razor Pages vs MVC controllers distinction or that the UI layout has changed significantly.

assertions.assertion: Response recommends scaffolding fresh Duende UI templates (e.g., dotnet new duende-is-ui) as one approach for the UI migration
assertions.rationale: Shows 'dotnet new install Duende.Templates' and 'dotnet new duende-is-ui'.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions that the existing MVC-based UI will still work after namespace updates but will miss newer UI flows like device flow, CIBA, or dynamic providers
assertions.rationale: Does not mention that the existing MVC UI still works or that it misses newer flows like device flow, CIBA, or dynamic providers.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions fixing API changes in controller or page model code (e.g., request.Client.ClientId instead of request.ClientId, or ScopesValuesConsented instead of ScopesConsented)
assertions.rationale: Only vaguely says 'Some IdentityServer APIs may have changed' without mentioning specific changes like request.Client.ClientId or ScopesValuesConsented.
